steps.title: Use the visual context
steps.content: The passage describes moss, pines, cypresses, and willows as beautiful things Mr. Bradford sees. In “this gorgeous display of nature,” the word refers to a visible show of natural beauty.

steps.title: Reject mismatched meanings
steps.content: Choice A means a copy, not a scene being observed. Choice B means hiding, the opposite of a visible show. Choice C means showy arrogance, a human trait that does not fit the landscape. The needed meaning is a visible exhibition. The answer is D.
